name: Route of the White Mojón – running circular from Piornal (PR-CC 14)
name_original: Ruta del Mojón Blanco – running circular desde Piornal (PR-CC 14)
region_intro: Jogging around Piornal offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Gredos mountains, situated as the highest village in Extremadura. The region features varied landscapes, from the lush Jerte Valley to rugged mountain areas, providing a range of running experiences. Trails often lead through woodlands, past dramatic waterfalls and gorges, and offer panoramic views of the surrounding valleys. The elevation of Piornal at 1,175 meters ensures routes with significant climbs and rewarding vistas. Best jogging routes around Piornal The most popular jogging route is Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete. This route is known for its scenic waterfalls and impressive views. Another top favourite among local runners is Cascada del Caozo Trail, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.3 km) path. This circular route winds through the Jerte Valley, offering views of the Caozo Waterfall. Local runners also love the Route of the Pilones – running circular from Jerte through the Garganta de los Infiernos, a 6.3 miles (10.2 km) trail leading through the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Jogging around Piornal is defined by mountain views, the Jerte Valley, and numerous waterfalls and gorges. The network of running routes offers options for various ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Piornal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 180 runners have used komoot to explore Piornal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Piornal?
A: There are over 19 diverse running routes around Piornal, catering to various fitness levels. These trails explore the unique landscapes of the Sierra de Gredos mountains, offering a range of experiences from moderate paths to more challenging runs.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging trails in Piornal?
A: While Piornal's terrain often involves elevation due to its mountain setting, there are routes suitable for those seeking a moderate challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991200556/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.1-mile (5.0 km) path known for its scenic waterfalls and views, offering a good option for those looking for a less strenuous run.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails near Piornal?
A: The running trails around Piornal offer a diverse range of terrain. You can expect paths that wind through lush valleys, rugged mountain areas, and beautiful woodlands. Some routes feature mostly paved surfaces, while others are more challenging, traversing scrubland and granite rock formations. The region's elevation ensures routes with significant climbs and rewarding vistas.

Q: Are there any running routes that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, the area around Piornal is rich in dramatic waterfalls and natural formations. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991200556/garganta-de-las-nogaleas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garganta de las Nogaleas Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is famous for its waterfalls. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990926132/cascada-del-caozo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the Caozo Waterfall. For natural pools, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990912807/route-of-the-pilones-running-circular-from-jerte-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Pilones&lt;/a&gt; leads through the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve, showcasing unique landscapes including the well-known Los Pilones Natural Pools.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Piornal area?
A: Absolutely. Several running routes around Piornal are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990926132/cascada-del-caozo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.1-mile (8.3 km) circular path through the Jerte Valley. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990912807/route-of-the-pilones-running-circular-from-jerte-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Pilones&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.3-mile (10.2 km) circular trail through the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the jogging paths?
A: Piornal, as the highest village in Extremadura, offers breathtaking panoramic views. Many trails provide stunning vistas of the Jerte Valley and La Vera. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5638307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrero de la Virgen Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot. Additionally, routes like the Mojón Blanco Route and Peña Negra Route lead to high-mountain landscapes with incredible natural viewpoints.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Piornal?
A: The running routes in Piornal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from reviewers. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural scenery including waterfalls and mountain views, and the variety of paths suitable for different ability levels. Over 180 runners have used komoot to explore the area's trails.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Piornal offers several challenging routes for experienced runners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990912807/route-of-the-pilones-running-circular-from-jerte-through-the-garganta-de-los-infiernos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Pilones&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.3-mile (10.2 km) trail with significant elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991207739/cueva-de-la-serrana-trail-run-from-garganta-la-olla-rt-01-rt-02" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva de La Serrana – trail run from Garganta la Olla&lt;/a&gt; is an even longer 6.7-mile (10.7 km) route, both classified as difficult.

Q: When is the best time of year to go running in Piornal?
A: The best time to go running in Piornal depends on your preference. Spring is particularly stunning when the Jerte Valley is famous for its cherry blossoms. During spring and summer, the region's woodlands, such as those found on the Chestnut Tree Route, provide welcome shade. The diverse landscapes offer beauty throughout the year, but be prepared for varied weather conditions in the mountains.

Q: Are there any specific natural attractions or landmarks to look out for while running?
A: Beyond the waterfalls and viewpoints, the region boasts several natural attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3243264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Pilones Natural Pools&lt;/a&gt; in the Garganta de los Infiernos Natural Reserve are a unique geological formation. You can also appreciate the ancient chestnut trees in the woodlands, which are particularly beautiful in autumn. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3287752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerte River&lt;/a&gt; itself is a prominent natural feature of the valley.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Jerte Valley?
A: Many routes around Piornal provide spectacular views of the Jerte Valley. As Piornal is situated at the crossroads of the Jerte Valley and La Vera, trails often offer panoramic vistas from various altitudes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990926132/cascada-del-caozo-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada del Caozo Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, winds through the Jerte Valley, offering scenic views, especially during the cherry blossom season in spring.
tips: The Route of the Mojón Blanco offers a very entertaining itinerary and views and highlights the Cuerda de los Cervigueros with wonderful views towards the entire Valley of La Vera.
